instruction: Turn around and walk to the end of the bed. At the end of the bed turn right and walk to the hallway. Once at the entrance way, walk through the hallway to the next room.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.

I'm noticing a wall nearby, possibly at a corner, with what looks like dark furniture or a bed frame in the lower left. Following the instructions, I should turn around first before exploring further.
I'm in a room with a bed and need to navigate toward it. The bed is positioned to my right, so I'm turning to get a better view of it and then moving forward in that direction.

I'm orienting myself to the room layout—the bed is positioned with its headboard on the right side and the foot extending toward the lower left. I need to move forward to reach the foot of the bed, then turn right toward the hallway that's visible in the middle-left area.

I'm orienting myself in the space—the bed extends to my right with its foot end near me, and there's an archway straight ahead leading to the hallway. I'll move forward to the bed's end, then turn right to head toward the hallway.

I'm at the foot of the bed now, trying to figure out which direction to turn. The archway to my left leads to the hallway, but I was instructed to turn right at the end of the bed. Let me reorient myself and see what's actually to my right from this position.
I'm positioned next to the bed now, with its foot end right beside me. Let me turn right to explore what's in that direction.

I've hit a dead end facing a wall with art instead of finding the hallway I expected—I must have overshot the turn or misjudged my position. Let me backtrack and try a different approach, maybe turning right or getting a fuller view of the space.

I need to reconsider the spatial layout here. The instruction to turn right at the end of the bed leads to a wall, not the hallway, which means I've been interpreting the direction incorrectly. The archway I see in the front-left must actually be the passage to the hallway, so "turn right" needs to be understood relative to my walking direction after moving past the foot of the bed, not from my current position facing it.

Looking at the geometry more carefully: the bed's foot is close to me now on the left side, while the head with the white metal headboard is against the right wall. If I haven't actually reached the true end of the bed yet, I need to walk further forward alongside it, past the foot, and then turn right to face toward the archway direction.

But turning right from my current position would face me toward the dresser wall, away from the hallway—that doesn't match the instruction. So maybe the "turn right" instruction was meant for an earlier position, or I should just move forward through the archway since that's clearly the hallway passage.
I'm not making much progress with my current approach—the bed might be blocking me. Let me adjust by turning slightly left to face the archway and try moving forward from there.
I overcorrected and now I'm facing the wrong wall. Let me adjust my direction to locate that archway.
Now the archway is centered ahead. Let me move forward toward it.
Moving forward toward the archway.
I'm not making progress with the current approach—something's blocking me, probably the furniture in the way. Let me adjust course and try moving around it instead.
I can see the archway more clearly now on the left side, so I'll adjust my angle and move forward to get a better view.
The position hasn't changed, so I might be blocked by the bed. Let me try shifting right first to see if I can get around it, then move forward.

I've shifted position and can now see the bed's headboard up close on my right, while the archway has moved toward the left side of my view. I'm standing near the foot of the bed at the corner, so I'll turn left to face toward the hallway.
I can see an archway appearing on the right, so I'll adjust course slightly and head toward it.
I'm getting closer to the archway and need to adjust my position to center it better before moving forward.

I think there might be an obstacle like a dresser or cart blocking the direct forward path, so the side movements aren't getting me much closer to the archway. Looking more carefully though, the cart seems to be on the left side rather than directly in the way, so I should try angling slightly right and moving forward more decisively.
The archway is expanding as I move forward through the space.
The perspective isn't shifting much, so I'm probably getting blocked by the bed on the right side. Let me rotate left more sharply to face the archway directly and get around that obstacle.

I'm noticing I keep returning to the same view around step 44, 62, and 72—looks like I'm stuck in a corner near the bed, possibly blocked by the dresser on my left. Let me try rotating fully around and stepping back away from the bed, then navigate around it more carefully toward the left side while maintaining distance from the dresser.

Wait, I'm actually at the archway now. Let me check what's further left of the dresser and turn substantially left to get a better sense of the room layout and where the windows are.

A full 180-degree turn reveals a different part of the room—a window and dresser instead of the bedroom panorama I saw before. This suggests I've actually shifted position somewhat and am now closer to the window wall than I was initially. I should keep turning to map out the full layout.
I'm noticing a chaise lounge and another archway with what appears to be a lit doorway leading deeper into the space, so I'll keep rotating to get a complete view of the room.

I'm back at the bedroom view with the archway, and I'm verifying the rotation math. From step 97 to 121, I've completed a full 360-degree rotation: the first 12 steps took me 180 degrees, then 6 steps for 90 degrees, then another 6 steps for the final 90 degrees, totaling 24 steps back to the starting view. The consistency checks out.

Now I'm analyzing the room layout more carefully—the bed with its headboard is against the right wall, the archway leads ahead to a lit hallway or kitchen area, and behind me is a window seat with another lit doorway to its right. Since I keep getting stuck when trying to move forward, I suspect the bed frame edge might be blocking me even though there's visual clearance. My plan is to reposition by turning left to face the archway directly, then attempt small incremental forward steps to detect any collision earlier and find a clearer path around the dresser cart.
I can see the archway on the right now, so I'll adjust the angle to center it better and then move forward.
I'm adjusting course and heading more directly toward the archway instead of the gradual approach.
The archway is now positioned ahead and to the left, centered properly. I'll proceed forward with careful, deliberate steps while staying alert to what's around me.
Moved slightly. Continuing forward.

The bed comforter visible in the lower right suggests I'm positioned right against the bed edge, which is blocking my forward movement. The archway ahead-left appears clear based on the cream carpet visible there, but something is preventing me from moving in that direction—possibly the bed itself extending further into my path than I initially thought.
I can see the archway is positioned more to the right now, with open carpet space between me and the dresser ahead. I'll move forward into that open area.
The cabinet's gotten bigger and closer, so the translation worked. Now I'm heading toward the archway with a slight right turn.

I'm noticing the robot isn't advancing through the loop properly—it keeps returning to the same view after turning right and moving forward. Let me test a longer sequence of pure forward movements without turns to see if the position actually changes step by step, since I suspect the forward command might execute without moving if it hits an obstacle like the bed, making it hard to detect actual progress.

I'm noticing this same frame keeps appearing at regular intervals—steps 44, 72, 97, 121, 145—which points to a repeating cycle. It looks like my turning and forward movements are canceling each other out, and I'm essentially trapped bouncing between collision boundaries in a confined area rather than making real progress.

The dresser cart is blocking my direct path to the archway, so I need to navigate around it instead of trying to push through. Since the cart sits slightly left of center and the archway is to its right, I should try turning right to approach from the bed side and slip past the obstacle that way.

I'm getting a clearer view of the space now—the bed's on my right with its headboard visible, and the archway is ahead to the left. The cart has shifted behind me, so I can continue forward and around it to reach the archway.

I keep hitting the same stuck view no matter what movement combinations I try—it seems like there's a fixed point in the environment where the navigation snaps back, possibly due to collision sliding or navmesh constraints near the bed corner. The simulator might be failing to move me when blocked by clutter, keeping me at the same discretized position while rotations still work, which is why I keep seeing this identical composition.

Looking at the cart's consistent size and position across these screenshots, the camera position really hasn't changed at all—translations are completely failing when I hit obstacles. Since the direct path forward keeps trapping me, I should try going around the other way through the left side past the windows and chaise lounge area. I remember seeing a lit doorway near that chaise lounge when I turned 180 degrees earlier, which might be an alternate exit if the center archway isn't working. Let me reconsider the full room layout: there's the original corner view, then after turning around I see the bed on the right with a seating area and windows on the left, plus that center archway leading to a dimly lit hallway with pictures. But there's also a second doorway near the chaise lounge that looked lit—possibly a closet or another hallway entrance—so I have at least two potential exits to explore. So the archway with the carved relief above it is actually near my starting position, not the destination—the real end of bed I need to reach is the chaise lounge area on the far side of the room. That means I've been heading the wrong direction; I should turn around and walk across the room toward the loveseat where the windows are, then turn right there to find the hallway entrance.
I'm moving toward the window and dresser area to locate the doorway, keeping the loveseat to my right.
I've moved closer to the corner and hit a dead end with the wall, pictures, curtain, and window. Now I need to turn right to locate the loveseat or chaise area and find the doorway.
I'm noticing the chaise lounge with orange pillows, and I want to rotate the view to the right to get a better look at the doorway and what's visible through it.

I'm realizing this glass cabinet area is the same one I saw before, not a separate hallway—it's all one compact space with the loveseat adjacent to it. That means the archway I identified earlier really is the only exit, leading to the hallway with the pictures on the wall, so my original navigation instinct was right.

The real problem is just getting past that furniture cart blocking the way. Let me head back toward that archway and find a route around the obstruction.
